images.caption: Figure 1:(a) Preference scaling with our Poly-DPO and ViPO-Image-1M dataset. (b) When training on a biased preference dataset such as Pick-a-pic V2, our Poly-DPO outperforms Diffusion-DPO in all evaluation dimensions. (c) Our proposed ViPO-Image-1M dataset can comprehensively improve the SD3.5-Medium.
Figure 2:Overview of our ViPO-Image-1M and ViPO-Video-300K dataset.
Figure 3:Summary of our Poly-DPO. By adjusting only one hyperparameter and introducing only two new lines of code, our Poly-DPO can handle preference datasets with three different data distributions.

Figure 4:Ablation studies with differentα\alphaon datasets with varying noise properties. While only the HPSv2.1 score is visualized for clarity, a similar trend is observed across all other evaluation metrics

Figure 5:Performance Comparison between VLM and Human Raters.Accuracy(or Agreement Rate) is defined as the frequency with which a choice aligns with the consensus label (majority vote among human raters, excluding VLM predictions).(a) Overall:The VLM (81.2%81.2\%) demonstrates higher consistency with the consensus than the average individual human annotator (74.7%74.7\%).(b) By Modality:The VLM significantly outperforms humans on images (84.0%84.0\%vs.74.9%74.9\%) but performs comparably on video tasks (71.7%71.7\%vs.72.2%72.2\%).(c) By Category:The VLM excels in most metrics likeAesthetic(95.0%95.0\%) but only struggles with temporalMotion Quality(55.0%55.0\%vs.67.2%67.2\%).

Figure 7:Gradient magnitude of Poly-DPO loss with respect to logits under differentα\alphavalues. The gradient|−(1−p)​(1+α​p)||-(1-p)(1+\alpha p)|adaptively controls learning dynamics based on confidencepp.α>0\alpha>0enhances gradients for medium-confidence predictions to combat noisy labels,α<0\alpha<0suppresses overconfident predictions to prevent overfitting, whileα=0\alpha=0(standard DPO) proves optimal for high-quality balanced datasets.
Figure 9:The UI inferface used for our human evaluation.

Figure 10:Training dynamics of Poly-DPO and Diffusion-DPO on the Pick-a-Pic V2 dataset. Both methods exhibit high training stability, with evaluation metrics steadily increasing to convergence without any signs of model collapse.
